label: 1821 Small purchase contracting authority

Amendments2018—Subsec. (a). , which directed amendment of “” by inserting “To promote efficiency and economy in contracting and to avoid unnecessary burdens, the Architect of the Capitol is granted authority to utilize special simplified procedures for purchases of property and services the aggregate amount of which does not exceed $250,000.” before “Notwithstanding any other provision of law—”, was executed by making the insertion in subsec. (a) of , div. H, title I, which is classified to this section, to reflect the probable intent of Congress.Pub. L. 115–2442 U.S.C. 1821section 1201 of Pub. L. 108–7

Effective Date of 2018 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 115–244, div. B, title I, § 133(b)132 Stat. 2936Sept. 21, 2018“The amendment made by subsection (a) [amending this section] shall apply with respect to fiscal year 2019 and each succeeding fiscal year.”

children.text: shall apply with respect to purchases and contracts for the Architect of the Capitol as if the reference to “$25,000” in paragraph (1) of such section were a reference to “$100,000”; andsection 6101 of title 41
the Architect may procure services, equipment, and construction for security related projects in the most efficient manner he determines appropriate.

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: b Effective date
children.text: This section shall apply to fiscal year 2003 and each fiscal year thereafter.
